attacking_style: Prefers calculated, delayed attacks at 20-25km to go after feigning weakness. Shows restraint on steep climbs (12%+), prioritizing strategic positioning over early aggression.
team_tactics: UAE employs risk-averse GC defense strategy, keeping Conti conservative even when lacking breakaway representation. Individual tactical autonomy to make calculated decisions favoring time over stage wins.
terrain_preference: Excels on descents where he can disguise form and launch surprise counters. Less effective on sustained steep gradients (12%+) where conservative approach limits explosive opportunities.
vulnerability: Overly cautious on key climbs, described as 'cagey' when aggression needed. Risk-averse mentality can cost breakaway positions and opportunities when bold tactics required.
key_patterns: Feigns weakness before counter-attacking at decisive moments (2 instances: descent recovery, cagey early positioning)
Prioritizes GC time gains over stage glory in tactical decisions (2 instances: two-up finish, breakaway management)
Conservative on steep climbs despite tactical openings, aligned with team's risk-averse strategy (2 instances)
Demonstrates tactical maturity beyond typical breakaway rider, calculated risk assessment evident throughout Grand Tours
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed signals with tactical intelligence evident (stage management, calculated attacks) but overcautious approach limiting results. Consistency without breakthrough performance.
